items.title: Where pdb lives in the debug-loop spectrum
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  P["print()"] --> L["logging"] --> PDB["pdb / ipdb"] --> IDE["IDE debugger (VS Code / PyCharm)"] --> R["remote debugger (rpdb, debugpy)"]
  style PDB fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a
items.caption: pdb is the lowest-friction step up from `print` — no extra setup, no tooling required.

items.title: pdb stepping model
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  A["current line"] -- n --> B["next line in same frame"]
  A -- s --> C["first line of nested call"]
  A -- r --> D["current function returns"]
  A -- c --> E["run until next breakpoint or end"]
  B & C & D --> A
items.caption: n / s / r / c are the four directions of motion — most pdb sessions use only these plus `p expr`.
